zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Clifton II於1332年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾克里夫頓時，他的父親Sir Gervais Clifton14歲，母親Margaret Pierrepont15歲。他與Agnes de Grey在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1376年10月16日在他的家鄉享年44歲。

nameMeanings.name: Clifton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the very numerous places called Clifton, from Old English clif ‘slope’ (see Cliff ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Cliff
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Cliff(e), Cle(e)ve, or Clive, from Old English clif ‘slope, bank, cliff’, or a topographic name from the same word. The Old English word was used not only in the sense of modern English cliff but also of much gentler slopes and frequently also of a riverbank or seashore.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Clifton II was born in 1332, in Clifton, Nottinghamshire, England, his father, Sir Gervais Clifton, was 14 and his mother, Margaret Pierrepont, was 15. He married Agnes de Grey in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 16 October 1376, in his hometown, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Clifton II est né en 1332, à Clifton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Gervais Clifton, avait 14 ans et sa mère, Margaret Pierrepont, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Agnes de Grey à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 16 octobre 1376, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 44 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Clifton II(이)가 1332년 영국 노틴그함실르 클리프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Gervais Clifton(은)는 14세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Pierrepont(은)는 15세였다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드에서 Agnes de Grey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1376년 10월 16일 그의 고향에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Clifton II родился в 1332 г. в местности клифтон, ноттингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Gervais Clifton было 14 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Pierrepont, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes de Grey в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 16 октября 1376 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 44 лет.
